displayTitle: The Machine Tests Every Piece. Who Tests the Machine?

beats.narration: An automated eddy current line feeds the product, judges every signal, and sorts it. No one is in the loop for each piece.

payload.title: AUTOMATION & FEEDBACK
payload.sub: The line tests every piece. Your job is proving it still can.

beats.narration: Eddy current automates better than any method. It never touches the part, the result is already a voltage, and the decision finishes fast.

tiles.s: No couplant, nothing drags at speed
The signal is already a voltage

beats.narration: Feedback keeps it honest. Double the line speed and the same notch makes a signal twice as fast that slides out of a fixed passband.

payload.heading: Speed shifts the signal off the filter
payload.note: ENCODER RE-CENTERS THE PASSBAND AT ANY SPEED

beats.narration: So feedback measures, compares, and corrects. An encoder tracks true travel, balance holds the null, and self-monitoring forces a dead channel to announce itself.

payload.heading: The loops that hold the setup

chips.s: A dead channel can't look clean

beats.narration: The machine makes the per-piece calls. You answer for whether it was working: verify on schedule, watch the loops, stop the line at any limit.

payload.sub: Feedback maintains the test. It never relaxes it.
